title: Intensely Peppery Middle-Eastern Pork Shoulder Clay Pot-Roast
description: A hearty, flavorful, slow-cooked pork shoulder dish infused with Middle-Eastern spices and a hint of heat.
instructions: Preheat the clay pot to 300°F (150°C) for at least 30 minutes before cooking.
Season the pork shoulder with salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in the clay pot over medium-high heat. Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer the pork shoulder to the Raclette grill and cook for 10 minutes on each side, or until slightly charred.
In the clay pot, sauté the garlic, parsley, and peppercorns in 1 tablespoon of olive oil until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the red wine vinegar and lemon juice to the clay pot and stir to combine.
Return the pork shoulder to the clay pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the clay pot to the oven and cook for 2-3 hours, or until the pork is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the pork shoulder from the clay pot and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Serve the sliced pork shoulder with the peppercorn sauce made from the clay pot's juices, and garnish with parsley.
Slice the pork shoulder against the grain and serve hot with a side of baba ganoush or your favorite dipping sauce.
Transfer the clay pot to the oven and cook for 2-3 hours.
